subject: math
title: Grade 5 Math — EOG Practice Test 1
instructions: Read each question carefully. For multiple-choice questions, choose the one best answer. For 'select all that apply' questions, choose every correct answer. For numeric-entry questions, write your answer as a number. For fill-in-the-blank equations, write the number that makes the equation true. Calculators are NOT allowed. You have 120 minutes.

questions.questionText: What is the value of 1/2 + 1/3?
questions.explanation: Common denominator 6: 1/2 = 3/6 and 1/3 = 2/6. 3/6 + 2/6 = 5/6. The distractor 2/5 incorrectly adds numerators and denominators.

questions.questionText: What is the value of 5/6 − 1/4?
questions.explanation: Common denominator 12: 5/6 = 10/12 and 1/4 = 3/12. 10/12 − 3/12 = 7/12. The distractor 4/2 subtracts numerators and denominators separately.

questions.skill: NF.5.2 add mixed numbers word problem
questions.questionText: A recipe uses 2 1/3 cups of flour for bread and 1 1/2 cups for muffins. How many cups of flour are used in all?
questions.explanation: 2 1/3 + 1 1/2. Common denominator 6: 1/3 = 2/6 and 1/2 = 3/6, so 2/6 + 3/6 = 5/6. Whole numbers 2 + 1 = 3. Total = 3 5/6 cups. The distractor 3 2/5 adds numerators and denominators.

questions.skill: NF.5.1 subtract mixed numbers with regrouping
questions.questionText: Maria has 4 1/4 yards of ribbon. She uses 1 2/3 yards. How many yards of ribbon are left? Enter your answer as a decimal rounded to the nearest hundredth.
What is the value of 2/3 × 3/4?
questions.explanation: Multiply numerators and denominators: (2×3)/(3×4) = 6/12 = 1/2. The distractor 6/7 adds instead of multiplying.

questions.skill: NF.5.4 multiply fraction by whole number
questions.questionText: What is the value of 3/5 × 10?
questions.explanation: 3/5 × 10 = 30/5 = 6. The distractor 30 multiplies only the numerator by 10 without dividing.

questions.questionText: A rectangular garden is 4 1/2 feet long and 2/3 foot wide. What is the area of the garden in square feet?
questions.explanation: Area = length × width = 4 1/2 × 2/3 = 9/2 × 2/3 = 18/6 = 3 square feet. The distractor 5 1/6 adds the two dimensions.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true. Write your answer as a fraction.
Select ALL expressions that have a value of 1/12.
questions.explanation: 1/4 ÷ 3 = 1/12 ✓. 1/3 ÷ 4 = 1/12 ✓. 1/2 ÷ 6 = 1/12 ✓. 1/6 ÷ 2 = 1/12 ✓. Each divides a unit fraction so the denominator becomes 12.

questions.skill: NF.5.7 divide whole number by unit fraction
questions.questionText: Tom has 5 cups of trail mix. He puts 1/3 cup into each small bag. How many small bags can he fill?
questions.explanation: 5 ÷ 1/3 = 5 × 3 = 15 bags. Each cup makes 3 bags, and 5 cups make 15 bags. The distractor 5/3 multiplies by 1/3 instead of dividing.

questions.questionText: A piece of rope is 1/2 meter long. It is cut into pieces that are each 1/8 meter long. How many pieces are made? Enter your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: 1/2 ÷ 1/8 = 1/2 × 8/1 = 8/2 = 4 pieces. On a number line, 1/2 = 4/8, so there are four 1/8 segments.

questions.questionText: Select ALL expressions that have a value equal to 3/4.

questions.skill: NF.5.4 multiply fraction by whole number
questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true. Write your answer as a whole number.
What is the value of 7/10 − 2/5?
questions.explanation: Common denominator 10: 2/5 = 4/10, so 7/10 − 4/10 = 3/10. The distractor 5/5 subtracts numerators and denominators (7−2 over 10−5).

questions.questionText: A class has 24 students. 3/8 of the students walk to school. How many students walk to school? Enter your answer as a whole number.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true. Write your answer as a whole number.
In the number 4.736, what is the value of the digit 3?
questions.explanation: In 4.736, the digit 3 is in the hundredths place, so its value is 3 hundredths (0.03). The 7 is tenths and 6 is thousandths.

questions.questionText: What is the value of 3.5 × 10³?
questions.explanation: 10³ = 1,000, so 3.5 × 1,000 = 3,500. The decimal point moves 3 places to the right. The distractor 35 moves only one place.

questions.questionText: What is the value of 2.45 + 1.7?
questions.explanation: Align decimals: 2.45 + 1.70 = 4.15. The distractor 2.62 misaligns (2.45 + 0.17).

questions.questionText: What is the value of 0.6 × 0.4?
questions.explanation: 6 × 4 = 24, and there are two decimal places total (one in each factor), so 0.6 × 0.4 = 0.24. The distractor 2.4 misplaces the decimal by one position.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
What is the value of 4.5 ÷ 9?
questions.explanation: 4.5 ÷ 9 = 0.5. Check: 0.5 × 9 = 4.5. The distractor 5 ignores the decimal.

questions.questionText: A factory packs 1,288 markers into boxes of 23 markers each. How many full boxes can be packed? Enter your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: 1,288 ÷ 23 = 56 exactly (23 × 56 = 1,288). So 56 full boxes can be packed.

questions.questionText: What is 7.165 rounded to the nearest hundredth?
questions.explanation: The thousandths digit is 5, so round the hundredths digit up: 7.165 → 7.17. The distractor 7.16 incorrectly rounds down.

questions.questionText: Select ALL statements that are TRUE.
options: In 22, the left 2 is 100 times the right 2.
In 0.77, the 7 in the hundredths place is 1/10 of the 7 in the tenths place.
In 4.4, the 4 in the ones place is 10 times the 4 in the tenths place.
In 55.5, the 5 in the tens place is 10 times the 5 in the ones place.
questions.explanation: Each place is 10 times the place to its right and 1/10 of the place to its left. Tens is 10× ones ✓; hundredths is 1/10 of tenths ✓; ones is 10× tenths ✓. In 22, the left 2 is 10 times (not 100 times) the right 2 ✗.

questions.questionText: A runner buys 3 energy bars at $1.25 each and a bottle of water for $0.95. What is the total cost in dollars? Enter your answer as a decimal.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
A rectangular box has a length of 5 cm, a width of 3 cm, and a height of 4 cm. What is the volume of the box?

params.label: base 5 cm by 3 cm, height 4 cm

questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height = 5 × 3 × 4 = 60 cubic cm. The distractor 47 comes from a surface-area mix-up.

questions.questionText: A storage bin is 8 inches long, 5 inches wide, and 6 inches tall. A second bin has the same length and width but is only 2 inches tall. How much GREATER is the volume of the first bin than the second, in cubic inches?
questions.explanation: First bin: 8×5×6 = 240. Second bin: 8×5×2 = 80. Difference: 240 − 80 = 160 cubic inches. (Or 8×5×4 = 160.) The distractor 240 is the first bin's volume.

questions.questionText: A rectangular fish tank has a volume of 144 cubic inches. Its base is 6 inches long and 4 inches wide. What is the height of the tank, in inches? Enter your answer as a whole number.
questions.explanation: Volume = L × W × H, so 144 = 6 × 4 × H = 24 × H. Therefore H = 144 ÷ 24 = 6 inches.

questions.questionText: A jug holds 2 liters of juice. Mia pours the juice into glasses that each hold 250 milliliters. How many glasses can she completely fill? (1 liter = 1,000 milliliters)
questions.explanation: 2 liters = 2,000 milliliters. 2,000 ÷ 250 = 8 glasses. The distractor 4 forgets to convert and does 1,000 ÷ 250.

questions.questionText: The data table represents a line plot of ribbon lengths measured to the nearest quarter inch. What is the TOTAL length of all the ribbons combined?
questions.explanation: Total = 2×(1/4) + 3×(1/2) + 1×(3/4) = 2/4 + 6/4 + 3/4 = 11/4 = 2 3/4 inches. The distractor 1 1/2 just counts ribbons above 1/4.

questions.questionText: Use the line-plot data table to answer. Select ALL statements that are TRUE.
options: The most common length is 1/2 inch.
More ribbons are 3/4 inch long than 1/4 inch long.
There are twice as many 1/4-inch ribbons as 3/4-inch ribbons.
questions.explanation: Total ribbons: 2 + 3 + 1 = 6 ✓. The 1/2-inch length has the most (3) ✓. There are 2 ribbons of 1/4 inch and 1 of 3/4 inch, so 2 = 2×1, twice as many ✓; and 3/4 inch has FEWER, not more, than 1/4 inch ✗.

questions.questionText: A rope is 3.5 meters long. How many centimeters long is the rope? (1 meter = 100 centimeters)
questions.explanation: 3.5 meters × 100 = 350 centimeters. The distractor 35 multiplies by 10 instead of 100.

questions.questionText: On a coordinate grid, a point is located 4 units to the right of the origin and 2 units up. Which ordered pair names this point?
questions.explanation: An ordered pair is (x, y): x is horizontal, y is vertical. Right 4 and up 2 gives (4, 2). The distractor (2, 4) reverses the coordinates.

questions.questionText: Which statement is ALWAYS true?
questions.explanation: A square has four right angles and opposite sides parallel and equal, so every square fits the definition of a rectangle. Not every rectangle is a square, and a rhombus need not have right angles.

questions.questionText: What is the value of 3 + 4 × (6 − 2)?
questions.explanation: Parentheses first: 6 − 2 = 4. Then 4 × 4 = 16. Then 3 + 16 = 19. The distractor 28 comes from adding before multiplying: (3+4)×4.

questions.questionText: Which expression means 'add 8 and 5, then multiply the result by 3'?
questions.explanation: Adding 8 and 5 first requires parentheses, then multiplying by 3: 3 × (8 + 5). The distractor 8 + 5 × 3 multiplies 5 by 3 first because of order of operations.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Pattern A starts at 0 and adds 3 each time: 0, 3, 6, 9, ... Pattern B starts at 0 and adds 6 each time: 0, 6, 12, 18, ... Select ALL statements that are TRUE about the patterns.
options: Both patterns will always have the same value at each term.
The ordered pair (9, 18) shows the 4th terms of Pattern A and Pattern B.
Each term in Pattern B is twice the matching term in Pattern A.
Each term in Pattern A is twice the matching term in Pattern B.
questions.explanation: Each Pattern B term is twice the matching Pattern A term: 6=2×3, 12=2×6, 18=2×9 ✓. Pattern A is half (not twice) Pattern B ✗. The 4th terms are A=9, B=18 → (9, 18) ✓. They are equal only at the first term ✗.
